claim: Among the published character/word mappings for Voynich (Brady Syriac, Schechter Latin-Occitan, Pagel trilingual), one produces a meaningfully higher connector-content bigram delta on Hand A alone than the others. That map ranks first as the candidate for Hand A's underlying language.
evidence_for: H-BV-HAND-A-LANGUAGE-01 showed Brady's Syriac map yields delta +0.0044 on Hand A alone — sign flipped positive from -0.0098 full-corpus
H-BV-HAND-A-INTERNAL-01 confirmed Hand A is linguistically structured; some map should produce better shuffle-test behaviour than random
Schechter's 4,063-entry glossary is the densest word-level map available and targets Latin/Occitan explicitly
evidence_against: Different maps use different connector sets; the ranking depends on definition choices
All three maps have already been shown to fail on full-corpus shuffle tests; none may cross +0.010 even on Hand A
Word-level maps (Schechter, Pagel) are architecturally different from character-level (Brady); comparing deltas is apples-to-oranges without caveat
test: For each of Brady (Syriac, char-level), Schechter (Latin-Occitan, word-level glossary 4,063 entries), Pagel (trilingual, word-level 81-term): apply the map to every Hand-A token to produce decoded-word sequences. Define CONNECTORS for each map via the map's own function-word class (Brady: {w,d,l,m,kl,km,dy,ky}; Schechter: top ~20 Latin/Occitan function words in glossary whose decoded value is in {et, in, de, ad, est, cum, ex, non, sed, per, vel, si, e, en, a, am, es, o, que, ni, lo, la}; Pagel: {y=et, s=est, chey=cum, sho=sume, ar=aer, al=alius, por=pro}). Compute conn-content bigram delta (in-order minus shuffled) per map. Shuffled baseline: pool Hand-A decoded tokens, shuffle, rechunk by original line lengths.
test_threshold: Report the ordered ranking by delta. The highest-delta map becomes the primary hypothesis for future confirmation testing (pre-registered with expanded vocabulary). A map with delta >= +0.010 would individually trigger CONFIRMED under H-BV-HAND-A-LANGUAGE-01 rules. Otherwise the ranking is the output.

tests_run.details: RANKING: 1. Pagel-trilingual delta +0.0118 CONFIRMED (match rate 23.9%%). 2. Brady-Syriac delta +0.0044 MARGINAL (match rate 45.3%%). 3. Schechter-LatinOcc delta -0.0608 REFUTED (match rate 78.9%% but strongly anti-syntactic). Pagel is primary candidate for Hand A underlying language, despite low coverage.

reasoning: Hand A passes the linguistic-structure test but Brady's map is only marginal. The question Brain-V should answer next is: among the characters maps in the literature, which one is most consistent with Hand A's syntax? A ranking — even without any single map crossing confirmation — identifies the strongest candidate for deeper investigation.
source: Brain-V 2026-04-16 pre-registration following H-BV-HAND-A-LANGUAGE-01 marginal result
